question_text: Two identical cars of mass 1000 kg each are on a hill 50 m high (g = 10 m/s²). Car A is stationary. Car B is rolling down at 10 m/s. Compare the total energy stored in gravitational and kinetic stores for both cars. Which car has more total mechanical energy and why?

options.label: A
options.text: Car A has more total energy because it is stationary and stable

options.label: B
options.text: Car B has more total energy by 50,000 J

options.label: C
options.text: Both cars have equal total energy at 500,000 J

options.label: D
options.text: Car A has more total energy by 50,000 J

correct_answer: B) Car B has more total energy by 50,000 J
explanation: Car A: GPE = 1000 × 10 × 50 = 500,000 J, KE = 0 J, total = 500,000 J. Car B: GPE = 500,000 J, KE = 0.5 × 1000 × 10² = 50,000 J, total = 550,000 J. Car B has 50,000 J more because it possesses both gravitational and kinetic energy stores simultaneously.
